exemplar.title: To what extent has high inflation negatively impacted stakeholders in Argentina from 2019 to 2024?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has high inflation negatively impacted stakeholders in Argentina from 2019 to 2024?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated, time-bound (2019â24) and consistently guides the investigation.
The topic is communicated accurately and its relevance to Economics is well justified.
Sections link back to the question, maintaining a coherent focus throughout.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria and does not explain how source reliability was assessed.
Reliance solely on secondary data limits methodological rigor (no primary sources or academic journals).
Minor wording lapses and some repetition reduce overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation exhibits a clear purpose and focus (two strands at band 5) but the methodology strand falls short of fully informed selection (band 3). Overall this equates to upper-half of the 3â4 band (score 4) rather than the top band, reflecting strong framing but only adequate methodological justification.

strengths: Source material is mostly relevant and applied to support quantitative calculations.
Economic terminology (CPI, AD/SRAS, Gini, FDI) is accurate and consistently used.
Clear macro-economic knowledge is demonstrated through coherent explanations.
weaknesses: Occasional misstatements (e.g., conflation in unemployment discussion) and inconsistent CPI data introduce inaccuracies.
Citation gaps (ân.d.â labels) and missing calculation steps weaken transparency.
Some figures are used illustratively rather than analytically, reducing depth of application.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Application of sources and disciplinary knowledge generally fits the 3â4 band, but recurring data inconsistencies, citation omissions and descriptive rather than analytical use of figures pull the criterion to the lower half of that band, warranting a score of 3.

strengths: Appropriate data series are selected for each stakeholder and clearly linked to the research question.
Analysis includes calculations (real income changes, inflation conversion) that go beyond pure description.
Argument structure is logical and sections conclude with supported judgments on impact.
weaknesses: Evaluation remains surface-level with limited discussion of policy alternatives or study limitations.
Causal chains sometimes skip steps (e.g., wage-price spiral) and contradictory claims appear (inflationâs effect on unemployment).
Critical depth is uneven; stronger data critique (statistical significance, context) is needed.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work exhibits a reasoned argument with clear, mostly supported analysis (placing it in the 4â6 band). However, lack of sustained critical evaluation, limited weighing of alternatives, and occasional lapses in logical rigor position it in the lower half of this band, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: The overall report follows expected sections (introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion).
Headings and subheadings are used to guide the reader.
weaknesses: Frequent formatting errors: inconsistent figure numbering, illegible axis labels, pixelated charts.
Citation mistakes (misspelled source names, misplaced headings) disrupt readability.
Page numbering is missing and bibliography entries are out of order.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the structure broadly follows conventions, pervasive layout and formatting issues severely impair clarity (band 1â2), and the minimal adherence to correct conventions warrants a score of 1.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated in the heading, is narrowly time-bounded (2019-24) and stakeholder-specific, allowing systematic investigation.  Subsequent sections (methodology, stakeholder analysis) consistently reference this question, evidencing a strong connection to discussion.  The phrasing âto what extent has high inflation negatively impactedâ¦â is focused, though the multi-stakeholder scope introduces some breadth.  Overall clarity and linkage satisfy the upper-band descriptor.

The topic â the impact of Argentinaâs recent bout of high inflation on key stakeholder groups â is introduced succinctly, its relevance to Economics is made explicit and the investigationâs purpose (to assess negative effects 2019-24) is clearly sign-posted.  Brief justification of significance (effects on livelihoods, policy insight) further underlines appropriateness.  Minor wording lapses and some repetition reduce polish, yet the focus remains unmistakable throughout, matching the descriptor for the 5-6 band.

Statistical series (CPI, GDP per capita, interest rates, FDI flows) and institutional reports are woven through the narrative and plotted in original charts.  These materials are mostly pertinent to analysing inflationâs effects and are applied to support calculations (real income, unemployment trends).  However, some figures are used illustratively rather than analytically, and occasional citation gaps or ân.d.â labels weaken precision.  This aligns with âmostly relevant and appropriateâ, meriting band 3-4, upper end.

The essay follows EE conventions: abstracted introduction, methodology, analytical chapters, conclusion and works-cited.  Headings and subsections help reader navigation and figures are sequentially numbered.  Minor ordering issues (two âFigure 6â) and some long sections without signposting are present, but overall the structure is clearly appropriate, satisfying the 3-4 band at a solid level (score 3).

Word count, title and subject are provided and within limits; figures are embedded and referenced.  In-text citations use parentheses but not always full APA detail, and page numbering is not visible in the excerpt.  Some charts are pixelated and axis labels occasionally illegible.  Thus layout considerations are partially met, falling in the 1-2 band (score 2).

Arguments are structured stakeholder-by-stakeholder and end with a succinct conclusion claiming impacts are âto a great extentâ.  Some evaluative elements appear (recognition of HDI limits, debt sustainability issues, differentiation between household income groups) yet critique is often surface-level and alternatives/policy responses are not weighed.  Consequently the discussion is reasoned but only partially critical, matching lower half of 4-6 band (score 4).

The student undertakes calculations, trend analysis and diagrammatic explanations that go beyond narration; for example, converting CPI to cumulative inflation, estimating real income changes, and linking deposit-rate volatility to FDI inflows.  However, chains of reasoning sometimes jump steps, and causality is asserted rather than tested (e.g., wage-price spiral explanation).  Depth is therefore solid but not comprehensive, placing it mid-band (4-6), score 5.

The student demonstrates solid macro-economic knowledge: explanations of demand-pull vs cost-push inflation, balance-of-payments links, Lorenz curve, wage-price spiral and HDI are generally accurate.  Misstatements are occasional (e.g., unemployment discussion conflates wage-price spiral with labour-demand effects).  Use of theory sometimes remains descriptive and lacks depth, yet overall understanding is clear and coherently expressed, fitting the 3-4 band.

Data series chosen are appropriate and timetabled to the research window; each stakeholder section draws on relevant indicators, indicating that  8 the majority of research is suitable and clearly connected to the RQ.  Nevertheless, coverage is unbalanced (government section relies heavily on one revenue/expenditure graph) and alternative viewpoints are scant, so the work falls short of the consistent excellence required for 7-9.

The essay relies entirely on secondary data (Trading Economics, BIS, Statista, IMF reports, news articles).  These are generally relevant and credible, but the range is limited (no academic journal work, no primary collection, few alternative viewpoints).  Source selection is partially justified (âraw unbiased dataâ, âvariety of perspectivesâ), yet criteria for inclusion/exclusion, reliability checks, and data-handling procedures are thin.  Thus methodology meets adequacy but falls short of the clear evidence of effective, informed selection demanded for 5-6.

Economic terminology (AD, SRAS, APL, CPI, Gini coefficient, FDI, nominal vs real, purchasing power) is used consistently and almost always correctly.  Diagrams are labelled with standard symbols, formulae for inflation and real income are applied correctly, and stakeholder categories are deployed using textbook language.  Minor typographical slips do not materially affect accuracy, supporting placement in the 5-6 band (score 5).

annotations.comment: The studentâs research question is precisely stated, time-bound (2019â24), and effectively guides subsequent analysis of stakeholder impacts, demonstrating strong alignment with the investigationâs focus.
The word count is clearly stated and remains within the 4,000âword limit, satisfying formatting requirements.

The introduction provides broad context on Argentinaâs inflation but conflates resource abundance with inflation causation without supporting evidence. Clarify and cite specific studies or data to strengthen background relevance.

Methodology lacks justification of inclusion/exclusion criteria and does not explain how source reliability was assessed. Consider adding criteria for source evaluation or integrating primary data to enhance methodological rigor.
The inflation calculation uses a CPI of 6,350 rather than 4,260 from January 2024 CPI data elsewhere, introducing inconsistency. Verify data sources and recalculate to ensure accuracy.
The reported 2,655 % rise in average price level is asserted without showing underlying CPI values or calculation steps. Include explicit data and workings to improve transparency of this analysis.
The figure citation â(Statisa)â is misspelled and lacks retrieval date or author information. Correct the source name to âStatistaâ and include full reference details.
Figure numbering is inconsistent (two âFigure 6â). Review sequential numbering and ensure all figures are uniquely and correctly labeled.
The paragraph on human development ends midâsentence. Complete the thought or adjust layout to signal continuation, avoiding reader confusion.

The HDI table is wellâconstructed, but the analysis that follows does not assess whether a â0.004 change is statistically or materially significant. Deepen evaluation by discussing the practical impact of this change.
CPI values are presented with inconsistent decimal precision across years. Standardize formatting (e.g., two decimal places) to improve readability.
The formula for real income omits clarification on whether inflation rate is a decimal or percentage. Define terms clearly and show stepwise substitution to avoid misinterpretation.
The percentâchange calculation appears to use inverted values (300.7 â 5,243.2). Apply the correct denominator (initial real income) and show full working.
Interpretation of the Gini coefficient is clear, but student should specify date of data collection to ensure relevance in discussion.
The text on unemployment contains contradictory causal claims about inflationâs effect on unemployment. Clarify the direction of relationship and support with evidence.
Heading level â4b2. Unpredictable Economic Environmentâ is formatted as H3 rather than H4, breaking consistency in the hierarchy. Adjust heading styles.
FDI table omits percentâchange value for 2024 and does not explain asterisk. Complete data or annotate missing entries.
Discussion of deposit interest rate volatility lacks time frame and data period in text. Specify the years shown to strengthen relevance to stakeholder impacts.
The FDI timeâseries table labels a 69 % increase in 2022 yet earlier text describes an overall drop. Reconcile description with data or amend percentage value.
Axes on the efficiency figure (firms at B vs A) are not labeled, making interpretation unclear. Add axis titles and units.
The parenthetical section header â(âArgentina Indicators,â n.d.)â appears as a heading rather than an inâtext citation. Relocate citation to the paragraph body for clarity.
Discussion of smaller firms spans a single long paragraph without subâheadings. Break into subsections or signpost key points to improve structure.
Figure on government revenue lacks a descriptive title and legend. Add clear labeling to distinguish nominal vs real revenue lines.
The expenditure figure is informative, but the text does not analyze why real expenditure falls while nominal stays flat. Provide deeper evaluation of policy implications.
Claims about public trust erosion and social unrest are generalised without data support. Incorporate survey results or protest incidence statistics to strengthen evaluation.
The conclusion synthesizes findings effectively but omits discussion of study limitations or policy recommendations. Adding these would enhance the critical evaluation.
Several works-cited entries lack consistent APA formatting and retrieval dates. Standardise citation style and include all required elements.
The UN HDR reference appears alphabetically out of sequence in the bibliography. Reorder entries alphabetically by author or organization.
